name: Swift Charts
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: Swift Charts
When using Swift Charts (import Charts):

CORRECT MARK USAGE:
- Chart { ForEach(data) { item in BarMark(x: .value("Label", item.x), y: .value("Value", item.y)) } }
- BarMark, LineMark, AreaMark, PointMark, RuleMark initializers take ONLY named parameters with PlottableValue via .value().
- These mark structs do NOT accept trailing closures. Apply modifiers as method chains AFTER the initializer.

MODIFIERS (method chain, NOT trailing closure):
- .foregroundStyle(by: .value("Category", item.category)) for color grouping.
- .foregroundStyle(.blue) or .foregroundStyle(Color.accentColor) for static color — do NOT use .foregroundStyle(.accentColor) (ShapeStyle has no member accentColor).
- .annotation(position: .top) { Text("label") } — annotation DOES accept a trailing closure (it is the exception).
- .symbol(by: .value("Type", item.type)) for point differentiation.
- .position(by: .value("Group", item.group)) for grouped bar chart.

COMMON PATTERNS:
- Grouped bar chart: BarMark with .position(by:)
- Stacked bar: Default when using foregroundStyle(by:) on BarMark without .position(by:).
- Date axis: .value("Date", item.date) — Charts handles Date formatting automatically.
- Custom axis labels: .chartXAxis { AxisMarks(values: .automatic(desiredCount: 5)) }
- Chart frame: .frame(height: 250) on the Chart view.

DATA MODEL:
- Chart data struct must conform to Identifiable.
- Pass raw numeric types (Double, Int) to .value() — never format to String before charting.
- Date values work directly — Charts auto-formats them on axes.

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- NEVER add a trailing closure to BarMark(...) { } — this causes "Extra trailing closure passed in call" compiler error.
- NEVER use .foregroundColor() on chart marks — use .foregroundStyle().
- NEVER pass formatted strings to .value() when the value should be numeric.
- NEVER nest Chart { Chart { } } — only one Chart wrapper.
- NEVER use .chartPlotStyle { } with content that produces ambiguous types — keep it simple.
antiPatterns: Adding trailing closure to BarMark/LineMark/AreaMark/PointMark — causes 'Extra trailing closure passed in call'
Using .foregroundColor() on chart marks instead of .foregroundStyle()
Passing formatted String to .value() when it should be a numeric type
Not setting .frame(height:) on Chart — defaults to tiny size
